prompt:
The FormValidator in validator.ts is sync-only and the signup screen needs server-side checks (username taken, promo code burned). I sketched the API with the frontend folks; test_async_dirty.ts is the agreed contract, please build to it.

addAsync(name, fn) registers async validators (value => Promise<string|null>) on an existing field. validateFieldAsync(name) runs the sync validators first and if ANY fail it resolves with those and never bothers the server; otherwise it runs all async validators, collects the non-null messages in registration order, stores them (getErrors(name), default []) and fires the onValidated(cb) listeners with (name, errors). The race is the whole point: a run that started earlier but finishes later, or whose field value changed mid-flight via setValue, is stale — its promise still resolves with what it computed, but it must not touch stored errors and must not notify.

validateDebounced(name, waitMs): trailing-edge debounce per field, each call pushes the deadline back, then it behaves exactly like validateFieldAsync. I got burned by fake-timer hacks in jest, so DO NOT reach for real setTimeout in tests — the constructor takes optional { setTimer, clearTimer } and the tests inject a manual clock. Defaults go to the real timers.

Also dirty tracking, the unsaved-changes banner needs it: addField takes an optional third arg initial (also the starting value); isDirty(name) compares current vs baseline, dirtyFields() lists dirty names in field order, markClean() re-baselines everything to current. Setting a value back by hand makes the field clean again.

Existing sync behavior is pinned by test_validator.ts — leave it passing, leave both test files unedited. node --test test_validator.ts test_async_dirty.ts is the gate.
